reviews.review_text:
[h2]Flashy Combos & Epic Primal Clashes[/h2]
If you are looking for an action RPG that skips the slow burn and gets straight to the spectacular, this game is an absolute must-play. Set in a beautiful, watercolor-esque world that feels right at home alongside titles like Tales of Arise, Granblue Fantasy: Relink delivers a beautiful and adrenaline-pumping experience. The main story campaign is relatively short, serving mostly as a 15 to 20 hour tutorial for the real game.

[h2]The Endgame Loop & Boss Battles[/h2]
Once the credits roll, the gameplay loop shifts entirely into a Monster Hunter style boss rush. You will team up with up to three other players or AI companions to take down massive Primal Beasts. The scale of these boss battles is breathtaking and exhilarating. The endgame grind is where you will spend the bulk of your time. Personally, aiming for 100% completion, grinding the optimal masteries, while crafting the Terminus weapons kept me hooked for hundreds of hours. The online matchmaking can be a bit finicky depending on who you match with, but grouping up with friends is an absolute blast. Even if you prefer to play solo, the AI companions in this game are remarkably competent. The addition of the post-launch DLC characters also added a lot of much-needed variety into the endgame farming loop, offering more characters and playstyles to experience.

[h2]Final Thoughts[/h2]
On a decent PC, the optimization is solid, offering smooth framerates that make the fast-paced combat shine. The Steam Deck handles the hub world fine, but massive boss fights will absolutely tank your framerate if you push the graphics too hard. Overall though, it is a phenomenal addition to the Granblue franchise. Relink is a blend of tight action combat, epic boss encounters, with an incredibly addictive endgame grind, and has been a highly rewarding ride from start to finish.

[quote][i]I write concise and in-depth reviews via the Steam Curator Program. If this was helpful, please consider following my Curator, [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/44230955-Narmaya-Onee-chan][b]Narmaya Onee-chan[/b][/url], for future recommendations.[/i][/quote]

This game KICKS ASS man. Is it tad repetitive? Sure. Is the story much to write home about? Not really. Is it an absolute blast to play and chock full of cool ♥♥♥♥? You bet your sweet ass it is.

It looks beautiful, the combat is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ aces, and the sheer variety and detail in the voice work is bonkers (seriously there's banter lines for every character combo you can do). There's so many cool and unique characters to unlock that it reminds me of a gacha game like ZZZ, but with none of the absolutely terrible things that make those kinds of games suck your wallet and soul.

I really, really hope this game is successful enough to warrant a sequel beyond the upcoming expansion, because I absolutely need more of this kind of interactive, fun, flashy anime BS in my life.

Grandblue Fantasy: Relink is honestly a sleeper hit. I've had it in my library for years, and while I enjoyed it I didn't really get it after a few hours.

Obviously I have since come back and my god, this is a fantastic game. The story is a fun adventure that is already feeling well established. The characters are all fun, each with distinct personalities and gameplay. And the actual game moves from fantastic boss fights, hordes that remind me of Dynasty Warriors at times, and some real depth in character builds.

I also JUST learned there's an expansion coming out in a few months. So if there was Ever a time to get into Grandblue, its now. A game that really does just get better the longer you play.
Very enjoyable game with interesting characters and combat

Very fun and great game. 
Some characters need some much needed buffs and quality of life added to their kit. 
It is absolutely worth playing this game and the upcoming DLC!

Hopefully they make more games like Relink in the future.

I dropped the game a while ago because i was mostly playing online multiplayer games. Recently tried it again, and it got me back into playing single player games. The story is kind of mid, but the gameplay is exceptional. Having a lot of fun with the game, and will continue to play more with the expansion on the horizon

it has monster hunter grind with final fantasy styled character progression and flashy fast paste combat. I'm loving it so far. if your a monster hunter fan and don't mind a bit of anime in your games then you should try it. also has a big dlc planned to release July 9th

This post is from a Monsther Hunter fan. Please take it with a grain of salt if you are coming from another perpective.
Game as it is is fun, I will definetly come back for some time to play Expansion. 
Fighting stages are much faster and less "Tactical". Music is nice, 
Few small issues:
More importance of gear and farm rather then skill.
Some fights are a bit cluttered
Visual clutter when 4 players beat on the boss, hard ro read sometimes -  i wish there was an option to make them more subtle.
Hitboxes are a bit off.

100% worth the purchase!

One of the -best- Action RPGs in recent memory, combat is extremely hectic; but still extremely fun!
Smooth as butter, especially with Co-Op, playing it with friends is just as good as solo.

Also, mmmm anime tiddy

I would say this game is comparable to the Monster Hunter series with a twist.  Game is fun both for casual play and for people who are looking for a challenge.  I stopped playing a year ago only due to declining number of players.  Then I picked it up again recently since I saw reasonable player numbers to have a full team with minimal wait.  I still love it, even after a long break!  Community is very nice and supportive!
Fun gameplay and a good story. Would recommend watching the anime before playing the game, but you can get into it without watching it as I did. Great PvE game.
Fast paced monster hunter on steroids with mmo boss mechanics. And i'm in love with it.
Fun combat and leveling/upgrading system. Pretty good selection of character to chose from.
Peak boss fights especially with Gallanza and Maglielle ♥♥♥♥ got me tweakin like terraria calamity boss runs 60 fps stable as well pretty optimized game.

This game is excellent and my only regret is not buying it two years ago. I had been keeping my eye on it for a long time but never pulled the trigger.

With the expqnsion announced I decided to jump in and I have really enjoyed it. Can't wait for all the new content.

This is basically anime monster hunter without a lot of the tedium (and I say that as a big MH fan).

Highly recommended if you like the idea of anime Monster Hunter.
The story is very fleshed out and the music is fantastic! The character designs are also very interesting.
The A.I controlled companions are surprisingly good.

My only gripe/warning is IF you buy this for multiplayer you should know that although multiplayer is VERY fun and enjoyable it doesn’t really become useful until after you finish the story. I’d almost claim that multiplayer is useless until end-game.
So first you do 12-18 hours of single-player and THEN you unlock end-game with over 50 hours of content you can do alone or preferably in multiplayer.
Great little fantasy game with awesome characters to pick from and a fun story.

What I wouldn't give to experience the story mode for the first time again.

End game content is pretty grindy but it's worth every penny to get it just for the single player part of it.
Its like anime Monster Hunter only the story is actually good and the combat is so much smoother.

Came to give it a try, stayed for the boss fights. Every time I thought I had become strong enough, the game introduced a boss that made me learn its mechanics all over again. Fast-paced combat, awesome characters, and an excellent soundtrack. Highly recommended.

The games really fun if your into co-op large scale boss fights. The game looks great, and the combat feels fluid and impactful. The only potential issue i could see people having is that to access the majority of the games quests and challenging content you need to finish the story mode and several quests, however when the new DLC launches there should be an option to skip to endgame.

If you're a fan of Monster Hunter and anime action RPGs then I highly recommend. Amazing combat, many unique characters that fit all kinds of play styles, enjoyable boss fights, with a short, but nice story. The endgame content does require a lot of grinding but if you enjoyed playing through this game you may find little to no complaints about it. Extremely solid game all throughout.

This game is very fun, but be prepared for the grind. As of writing this, I am still yet to reach the bosses I've only heard people talk about and have only managed to reach max offense and defense masteries of about four or five characters, of about 23 (and that number is gonna go to 29 with the new expansion, very excited for that.) This a good game, try it

Story is trope-loving, fantasy slop. Every stereotype front to back. Thankfully, it's only 10 hours long (not counting post game chapters). I could spend hours ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ on it, but the actual gameplay loop is very fun and exciting. The monster designs aren't too stale (variants are present, but idrc) and they each have a respectable move set. Instead of class systems the game presents 20 characters with individual skill trees, weapons, and playstyles. I very much prefer that than playing the same 6 classes present in every fantasy game. Very nice game. Sadly most of the fun is locked behind post game, but so is monster hunter so who cares.
Incredibly fun and addicting game loop! Great bosses and playable characters. I'm glad I picked it up when I did! Catching up and grinding for the upcoming expansion!

Honestly, Went into this game expecting a semi cringe story and not having played any Granblue games. BOY WAS I WRONG. The game play for the story was decent, the end game felt like monster hunter, and the combat gets harder as you go up difficulty. "Yea its supposed to be harder, your going to higher difficulties." WRONG, this game pushes you thinking about when to use skills and when to burst damage with while making sure you learn how to dodge your opponent's attacks. I had a blast playing this game and streamed it live on Twitch for the story and it was worth my $40 hands down.
Once you understand the combos and abilities the gameplay is very satisfying.
Bought the game 2 days ago, love this - the combat, the visuals. amazing

Yes. One of the best games ever made. Hugely underrated.
If you always dreamed to play in a universe that is as close as possible to the fantasy RPG worlds or Isekais you see in Anime. Then THIS is it.
Btw... Just saying... But this game has probably one of the absolute best and strongest foundations to rival WoW or FFXIV if it was made an Open World MMORPG. (Dear devs, please make it happen <3 ^^)
Really fun Monster Hunter-like esque game. Gameplay is very smooth and all the animations and music are great. Highly reccomend

excellent game. if you like monster hunter and phantasy star online 2 this game is for you.  fun and easy gameplay. you dont need to be a pianist to dish out that hard dps. beautiful graphics.  most of my hours is afk grinding to get top tier items. most is soloable. and once you get the right stuff you can afk everything except for the last 5 missions. and im an idiot and achieved this.  cant wait for the expansion and hope to see you there.

Great variety of characters, fantastic combat, good story. Loved how it plays like a Monster Hunter game with the boss rush levels, and how intricate the build crafting can be. Looking forward to the expansion!

I am new to the Granblue series. You can play this game without any knowledge of the previous games although I am sure it would make the experience better. Great game, game play, and an incredible soundtrack as well. Game also has co-op as well which is always a plus. Just started playing again in anticipation for the next big update in July.
gameplay is fun, and character kits are well designed. Multi boss fights are kinda scuffed though, and min-maxing in the late endgame is too grindy.

So far the games runs fine on my PC, since there's no noticeable changes in the graphics from standard to ultra i can enjoy the game with standard graphics.

Now that i have finished the story of the game, i can say that the story is what i'm expecting from a JRPG especially from Granblue Fantasy it's not the best compared to other JRPG but also not the worst but it fits to my liking: easy-to-understand dialogue, straightforward storytelling, beautiful character art, with a bit of onion in the story. And with the new DLC coming out later i hope it will bring the continuation of the story from the base game, i can't help but wanting to see more of their journey, it's like hearing your favorite anime is getting a season 2 (lol).

Coming from a Monster Hunter title game, I can see why people calling this game similar to monster hunter games. When taking quest for example, you go to a quest counter and and choose the quest you want to take, but instead of going solo in MH if you're playing single player in this game you can form a party with a Computer AI that controls other character you have in the party. One of the reason i like this game is in the combat mechanics: SBA, and Link attack.

Skybound Art (SBA):
Think of it like the ultimate of the character, and the ultimate can be combined with your party member to trigger 2-Combo, 3-Combo, and Full-Combo, 2-Combo meaning only two of your party member are performing SBA so on and so forth and each SBA combined with your party member will trigger a unique skill based on your initial SBA, So there's so much combination and team comp that you can experiment.

Link Attack:
Link attack is the flashy move here, it is triggered when the 'monster' is stunned. Well i don't really know if there's a additional requirements for the link attack to be triggered i just click a button and let the magic happen.  

So, this game is perfect for you if you like Monster Hunter games and want to play similar game, and if you like flashy combat you will enjoy the combat mechanics in this game and the MM0-like attack indicator for the enemies.

This game is an absolute hidden gem of a game, the boss fights are S tier, amazing visuals, good patterns and very lovely gameplay, this RPG skips all the usual slop you'd find in the genre and gets you straight to the flashiest and coolest fights against some amazing looking bosses, i only did 3 bosses and everytime i was in awe. 
Would recommend for people who like flashy fights against cool bosses.

The fact i haven't left a review earlier is a sin, this game is FANTASTIC if you like JRPGs, FF games, Monster Hunter, or just action games you can sink your time into. this is one of the few games for me is a 10/10.
Very cool epic music and boss battles and even viable to play completely solo

Hello fellow gacha addict, rest your weary legs and try this game out. If you want an RPG with a sizeable cast of unique characters with a healthy mix of waifus AND husbandos with incredible designs and varied combat styles, character backstory quests, material grinding and upgrading in the vein of Monster Hunter, but won't drain your wallet every 3-6 weeks, this is literally the only game like that that I know of. It is, quite literally, my dream game. I'm excited for the upcoming DLC.

I bought this game on console when it first came out. I got it again on PC to play with my friends and to help them out too. Sure, I have to start all the way over, but I don't mind the grind. This game is perfect for those of fans who are monster hunter lovers as well as for gamers who also love the grind.

beautiful game you can easily sink hundreds of hours into solo or with friends or random people online
i love this game and cant wait for the expansion
(i have a over around 2 hundred hours in this game on playstation)

Granblue Fantasy: Relink is a phenomenal action/Jrpg that combines the quest progression systems of Monster Hunter with the style and combat of a Ys game. A fantastic game that I picked up on a whim which has turned out to be an incredible surprise. Whilst I don’t care about the story at all and find a large number of the supporting characters to be utterly atrocious, the strength of this game's combat, playable characters and graphics really elevates it to the status of one of the best jrpgs I’ve played in recent memory. 

Granblue is apparently an existing IP that started life as a mobile gacha game which then also had a few fighting games (from the Guilty Gear devs no less). Relink is the result of years of delays, a game that was announced back in 2016 as a follow up which would take on the form of a more traditional JRPG. With support from platinum games for a while (after they then moved on despite some devs jumping ship to help with Granblue) we finally had a release in 2024…which I completely ignored. I went through a phase of not playing jrpgs due to the oversaturation and often low quality. 

Story-wise this game isn’t anything that special and I was a bit worried when I started playing the game, it drops you into the world with an assumption you know what’s going on, it also has one of the worst mascot characters I’ve ever experienced in a jrpg, you think Morgana is bad? Hell I’d even take Lisa simpson from Ni No Kuni 2 over what is possibly the worst character I’ve had the displeasure of listening to, that’s right I’m talking about Vyrn. Oh my god the english dub is impossible unless you have a high tolerance for whiny, cringe juvenile drivel. The original Japanese voiceover does somewhat alleviate this problem but I can still read his utterly disgraceful dialogue, and he never goes away.

But ultimately I don’t care about the story, it’s mainly an excuse to introduce you to the gameplay mechanics and the bosses you’ll be grinding. It lasts 10 hours and is good enough despite Vryn. Id who is basically Nero from Devil May Cry is a genuinely fantastic antagonist with a fantastic arc that made playing the story worthwhile.   

The star of the show is the gameplay, think Monster Hunter but with some other gameplay sprinkled in, you fight hordes of enemies as well as spectacular bosses, you grind, you level up your weapons and you do it again, over and over. If you do not like grinding games this probably will not win you over, it’s not worth playing for the story alone so be prepared for a long grind. 

The variety in this game comes from the large cast of characters, you unlock them as you progress but will have them all by about 20ish hours, they all have varied and unique gameplay meaning you will find someone you like, they range from my personal favourite - Vaseraga, a big tank of a man wielding a scythe who wouldn’t be out of place in Darksiders 2, to little anime ladies with horns and katana swords for all your discord mod needs. Every character is fun, versatile and great to play as. Combat is very deep as well, you of course have your standard attack as well as a unique attack that can be combo’d, looking at Vaseraga again he has the typical character action game combos of X, X, Y but then you can also hold down Y during these attacks to power them up all whilst a bar charges that’ll give you unique buffs, this isn’t to mention the special abilities you can put on a radial menu that can range from more damaging attacks, life steal, buffing the team and this can all be incorporated into your combo above, and this is all just Vaseraga who the game suggests is the easiest to play. 
This can all be done in Coop of course but I’ve only played this game solo, the game's bots are fantastic and are able to hold their own even against the late game content. With new DLC due out soon adding a ton of stuff I can see this game getting a massive boost in players soon. 

Really other than the story my only complaint is that the game is very grindy, I don’t mind this but for a grindy game it gets pretty egregious. 

In short this is a fantastic game, if you’re a gameplay first kinda person this game is for you, it has a decent enough story and fantastic platinum-like combat that’ll keep you coming back for more with its excellent character roster and spectacular boss battles. It might be a grind but it’s one you’ll enjoy (almost) every second of.

Fun action game with a neat story and characters. LOTS of grinding at the end game though. This could be a plus or a con depending on the person.

It's like old phantasy star online or monster hunter missions style and most of the grind will just be arena fights with bosses.

Fun game with enough replayability,
Story not too deep and has good pacing.

7/10

A very fun, very grindy game. 

Story is pretty short, extended out by the hunts they make you do between story missions, majority of your playtime will be spent farming end game raids for better Sigils. 

if you're not into grind heavy games, this game won't be your thing, however, if you do like grinding, flashy combos, and big numbers. This is absolutely the game for you.
Basically anime Monster Hunter. Really satisfying progression maxing all characters.

Peak third-person action game.
Combat is so good and satisfying !

GBFRelink is an excellent game with a lot of mechanical depth, every character is well crafted and has its own set of tools and complexities that you have to get through in order to master them, with the end result being very satisfying both in gameplay loop and effectiveness; the combat itself is very responsive as well as easy to pick up, but with a high skill ceiling (unless you use Sandalphon...). However it has a TERRIBLE problem with RNG drops and abysmal rates for some core features (namely sigils), both of which will comprise the entirety of the endgame (farming for min maxed units is the endgame, be it sigils or upgrade materials). The game doesn't offer much in terms of postgame either, barring a couple boss rush quests and a super boss, making it feel quite empty, On the other hand it's also severely lacking in terms of QoL, even something as basic as copying a session ID requires a mod.

These things hold back the game quite a lot, and while I still recommend it, I wouldn't suggest it to anyone who's either unable or unwilling to farm the same quests over and over for a mind numbing amount of time until you can get a singular drop of whatever material you might require; here's hoping the upcoming DLC will fix some of the problems I've highlighted previously.

[h3] If you like Monster Hunter Games, you will like this game![/h3] 

A pretty good JRPG/Action RPG game, that has everything you basically want in a game of this Genre.

The combat for me was one of my main highlights but it also comes with a cool story, cool Boss Fights and a reasonable progression system.
Really solid action rpg with an extensive endgame. Hopefully the devs continue to support the game after the expansion with more characters and missions.

Just buy it, more anime games need to upgrade to this level. Most anime games are stuck in the past. This is quality. The only frustrating part about this game is lack of key binding but there's a workaround with softwares such as hihide for example if using a controller which I recommend. Warning, story spoiler ahead...again spoiler ahead...what frustrates me is that they used the 'we steal your waifu and now you gotta come get us' trope. I hate that so much because it makes me wanna play to get back the main waifu but at the same time theres not much else to it. There's no real story...it's just a revenge bait of sorts. Eh, I give it a pass since the original story is like that anyways where everyone just wants Lyria for themselves.

Hope ya have a good time, enjoy!

This has got to be one of my favorite rpgs of all time, the combat is not overly complicated but not redundent either, the team building actually matters, upping characters isn't as simple as raising their level, the story is not a masterpiece but it's very good albeit a bit short (15 to 20 hours), 70% of the game's content lies in the quest system, short missions with different type of objectives, the last ones are very fun, especially the bosses

Good game, good variety of quests and characters with varying playstyles. 

Endgame missions are fun albeit grindy if you want to min-max builds which may be a turn off for some and a turn on for others (like me) 

Overall a good PSO/MH like style game with a ton of replayability if you like grind.

Expansion soon and it looks fantastic!

camera is god awful, targetting system makes no sense, visuals cannot be toned down so depending on your teammates they just cover the boss attack fx with their own attacks making it impossible to see.

apart from these issues its a decent game to grind but its just so frustrating to play with these. hopefully there are mods to fix these.

Game is too easy even on hard mode, and the difficulty seems to just spike up the sponginess of enemies. I'm told I should get through the end game, but then it seems like the difficulty is ultimately just grinding. I really like the different play styles of the characters, but something about the progression just feels really bland. In MHW and Diablo at least you have some cosmetic changes with each armor, whereas here you're just getting bigger numbers to get bigger numbers. I can see why people love this game, but it's far too linear and the combat loop of most characters just seems way too boring to fight an enemy that poses little to no threat for 5-10 minutes.

The best graphics I've ever experienced of this genre. I love playing my favorite GB characters like Narmaya in both GBFVR and this game. Different perspectives too from a fighting game camera and a rpg camera.

But the combat is so horrible and mashy theres no point in playing. It's a shame too. But the combat is the spine of any action game.
Rich story with a lot of individual character development, and a fun and engaging combat style
Stunning graphics and good enough story to keep you playing
Bought it on Steam after finishing it on PS5, and I can't get enough!  This feels like Monster Hunter once the campaign is finished and I can't recommend it enough!
The graphics and game play is gorgeous however it is a very boring snooze fest.

CBD score 4/10

It's a shame.

I played expecting to have to do the solo grind to the end but expected the end game to have players where it didn't & the Quick Match is fake.

Combat while typical J-RPG is smoother than most in the genre & the boss's were fun BUT... Once you get to the end game you start to see how dead the game is. The Quick Match is fake, you press it & it takes you an alternative way to set your game up as the lobby just the same as oh yeah the option above to set up your game as the lobby! The search is dead, I've put in multiple days now & found no Multiplayer match or being able to join a Quick Match! I got the game as it said it offers great co-op but it was just people trying to put out videos to get more players since the game is dead!
It has those typical J-RPG 90's cameras that the world moved on from but they stick with in truly epileptic fashion getting dragged all over the place even when you have all the cam options for movement off!

Overview:
I would of loved to recommend this game but it's dead & I can't. If you can get a great deal then the story is worth a play but your looking at under £20 worth of game, the side quests can fill it out but your not looking at much, then there's the end game grind which is grindy & there's no one to play with. If the game had a better community it would be worth sticking around but as it stands I can't recommend a dead game & then with the other minor issues it makes it not worth the time or effort..
I won't be bothering with the next update/dlc (June), especially if it's paid since it's already dead & there are better games at this price range that are worth the time.

[h1] Grind for the sake of grind [/h1]

Let me preface it by saying that this is NOT a terrible game... When it works.
When it doesn't, and that is a lot of the time, its a very bad MMO/Gacha/ARPG game wannabe.
With an excessive amout of pointless grind and bad game desigh decisions.

[h2] Story, Graphics, music and animations [/h2]

I will be honest, music, and anything involving graphincs or animations in this game are great, artstyle included. Nothing more to say here, honestly, its just great with no 'buts', but thats only the visual and music part. Gameplay wise,well... We'll get to that.

Storywise, however, this is typical JRPG with finding cats,saving world and fighthing gods. Pretty sure you have played something like this already. Not to say that the story is bad, it's just very plain. Can be entertaining for about 10 hours, but i would not recommend buying this for story at all.

[h2] Characters, builds and difference in playstyle [/h2]

Remember that i wrote "difference in playstyle" just a couple of words back? You can forget it! You can only be DPS, or of you [i] really [/i] want, you can pick 1 support skill (out of 4 per loadout).
Oh, also, there is grand total of 8 skills for most characters, half of which are useless, so you know, you don't really have much choice most of the time. AND most of the skills are variations of 'forwards charge attack', 'backwards move with an attack', 'big AOE in a circle around you' or a 'multiple hit combo'.
Sure, characters do have unique mechanics and movesets. but... They are not that unique, usually.
Oh, and ultimates all do the same thing just with different animation. 

Builds wise, you have 15 slots for artifacts/sigils/trinkets/etc. The problem is, at least 5-7 of them will always slot the same type of sigil, "Damage cap" and "War Elemental" and a characters specific sigils . Because without them, you hinder your damage output very badly. Oh, and most of the endgame bosses oneshot you anyway, so forget about tanks builds! Not to mention the also have attacks that are DPS checks.
Yep, you can only play DPS here folks, other classes don't exist here even on paper.

Talking about characters, good chunk of them have buff or debuff skills! The only problem is, you cap out on damage stats without them regardless, so they are pointless. Healing also kinda pointless, because oneshots. Debuffs can be good, IF you can apply them to the boss to any effect and they don't immediately become immune to damage (we will get to that...). So the only ones actually useful are those that either give you immunity to damage, damage resistance or can ressurect(if you play MP).

[h2] Endgame content and bosses [/h2]

So, you completed main story? You want more interesting and cool boss fights? Well, unfortunately for you, we only have 77 dragons that escaped from Elden Ring and reskins from the main story!
[b] I wish i was joking [/b]

You have seen about 90% of the game's content (not counting reskins) the second you completed main story. There is about 10+ quests where game forces you to fight the same dragon with different color of paint and slightly different attacks... That is, when that dragon is [u] alone [/u]...
Yes, the game on the last difficulty levels decided that we have not had enough random things on screen, and if places 2, 3 bosses you fought prior in the same room. And you will sometimes have to fight them more than once, because grinding for materials, remember?

Oh, also, bosses sometimes can become immune to damage and start throwing a lot of random stuff at you. Can you imagine what happens when there is 3 bosses instead of one? And this immune to damage phases can last up to 1 minute. FUN.
That's not to say that there is no good fights in the game, but its the same problem as the souls game, AKA the best fights in the game are against human-like opponents, while most of the game is full of lame giant monsters. [spoiler] Id, Maglielle, Gallanza and Bahamut fights are all great [/spoiler]

There is also a problem of visual clutter. Sometimes there is so many numbers and special effects on screen that you can barely conprehend where you are, much less what is going on.

[h2] Misc. [/h2]

There is also a lot of pointless, boring grind (thanks to bosses) for EVERYTHING. Weapon levels, sigils, sigils levels, etc. 

The little dragon is also somewhat annoying, but to his credit, at least useful and not as bad as PAIMON. (Sincerest condolenses to everyone who understands that).

A lot of early game quests are pure filler, they are pointless.

No coop in main story.

Optimisation is fine, had no problems.

[h1] Closing thoughts [/h1]

This is honestly a fun game when it wants to be. Unfortunately, most of the time it wants you to grind reskins of dragons or other giant monsters that are very boring to fight.

Presentation is top notch, and the story is entertaining, so if you want a enjoyable 10 or so hour game, i guess this may be worth it.

Otherwise, its probably better to pick something along the lines Nioh 2 or MGR:Revengeance for good ARPG. 

Play good games and goodluck, everyone.

6.5/10.

Endgame is made around beating multiple bosses in the same time while they clearly weren't designed that way. Difficulty for difficulty sake. Shame. It does have potential. The story was pretty good. But if you want to grind the endgame like you would in likes of MH it becomes unbearable. The online is dead so you have to level up your 4 AI companions aswell which is 30+ hours grind each. They are pretty dumb too so good luck. The Sİgil system(decorations in MH) is broken ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ and everyone who plays the endgame savescums in order to get good sigils and I can't blame them. Its that bad. Somehow worse than MH. Overall if you want to play the story its pretty fun but the endgame is made to steal your time. If thats why you are getting in stay away.

I don't think I can overstate just how bad the localization is.
Some examples in the past 15 minutes include changing "let's go, (character)" into "Wanna bust some ghosts?" and "no" into "Perhaps."
Characters are noting the strange energy in dub, but are asking if a character is alright in sub.
The game also had censorship that is at times odds with artwork already present in the game.
If you're the kind to sup on soy, I'm sure you'll be clutching your funkos with joy.
Unfortunately I am normal, so I can't recommend it based on the changes alone.
Very very very repetitive main story. The same 3 boss fights recycled ad nauseaum.
Game good but delete Vyrn and add Galleon instead also bonus points for the upcoming Ragnarok DLC

This game is the modern gaming companion yellow paint joke; for the first 30 min every action I performed was repeated by 3 or 2 NPC characters. Every treasure I found, I heard "Oh wow, a treasure" from NPC 1 and "Let's look at that treasure" from NPC 2, and this was for every little thing/action on screen. There was no room for me to experience or look at the game because the game was constantly telling me what to do or what I just did.

The game is so good that I have sinked 100 hours on it (Just played again recently). The grind is what you can expect on a JRPG but there is a limit cygames. I have been farming Proto Bahamut for so long, roughly 70 times now and I still have only managed to get 1 weapon to drop out of my 9 characters(didnt unlock the rest for smaller pool on terminus drop). Please release a pity system for rare drop materials in the upcoming expansion. AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH.

Update: did about another 40 runs and i got 2 more terminus.

Update: After playing again the grind is fine and all. But drop rates sucks especially if you have my luck which is basically non-existant. In the endgame most of the boss you need to farm and fight are 50% cutscene, 25% fighting and 25% dodging. Which is fine if your fighting the bosses just a handfull of times but when you have to fight them a hellish amount it starts to just drag on the fight and make it such a bore. There is some cutscenes that are fun at the beginning but it starts to get annoying later on. Who needs to watch their team AI do their ultimate a million times? Pls make it skippable. Who wants to watch the boss intro again and again when repeating the same quest? Again make it SKIPPABLE! There is also the issue regarding inconsistency with the AI performance sometimes they will do well but sometimes they literally just make the quest impossible to beat. Hope this once again gets fixed or metigated in the upcoming DLC.

Feels like a JRPG where you don't have to wait your turn. Dialogue can get a little hectic during the already flashy combat, but that's because each character has unique voice lines for interacting with multiple other characters from the large roster that your team can be built from! Well worth turning down the effects and music volume a bit in order to hear the entertaining party chatter.
With cross play coming no game will be able to compare

Pros: It's MH but flashier
Cons: It's MH but with WAY more grinding.

Fun game. 

Awful English localization.

I bought the game 2 years ago when it came out and played it only for about 20 minutes. it wasn't until I saw the influx of YouTube videos about the game that I decided to pick it up again and it turns out this is a very good game. I enjoy the Monster Hunter series and instead of continuing to emulate the older MH games over and over again I saw a video talking about relink specifically targeting MH players. Relink has that similar quest grind loop that I enjoy from MH but with a more relaxed feel, simple combat, big skill trees and "gacha" mechanics where the only price is playing the game. All the characters feel good and are viable which is nice. You can go all out and min max or play your way and get through stuff and all the DLC characters can be acquired through grinding which is neat. Main story is standard JRPG stuff but it's short and sweet so that's cool. I sunk 20 hours into this game over 2 sessions so if you're considering buying it you probably should.

Fun game, in the style of Monster Hunter, but with more weapon options (in the form of different characters).  The game can be soloed and the bot players are pretty smart.  I've tried playing online, but there doesn't seem to be people on to often.  I would suggest finding a friend who likes this type of game and playing together if you really want the coop portion of it.
